claimText: NASA's Lucy Mission is the first space mission designed to explore the Jupiter Trojan asteroids.
evidenceExcerpt: Lucy Mission (NASA) is the first space mission to explore Jupiter Trojan asteroids.
examUse: Match-the-following or correct-pair questions linking space missions to their primary targets.
distractorHooks: Attributing the mission to ESA or ISRO
Claiming the mission targets Saturn's rings or the main asteroid belt instead of Jupiter Trojans

claimText: The Tianwen-2 probe by China (CNSA) is designed to collect samples from the asteroid 2016HO3.
evidenceExcerpt: Tianwen-2 (China) aims to collect samples from asteroid 2016HO3.
examUse: Statement-based questions on recent asteroid sample return missions and their respective space agencies.
distractorHooks: Claiming Tianwen-2 is a Mars or Lunar sample return mission
Attributing the mission to JAXA or NASA

claimText: The European Space Agency's (ESA) Biomass Mission utilizes a P-band Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) to map global forest biomass and stored carbon.
evidenceExcerpt: Biomass Mission (ESA) carries a P-band SAR to map global forest biomass and stored carbon.
examUse: Statement-based questions on Earth observation satellites, their specific payloads (P-band SAR), and environmental applications.
distractorHooks: Attributing the mission to NASA or ISRO
Claiming it uses optical sensors instead of SAR
Stating its primary purpose is mapping ocean salinity or polar ice

claimText: LUPEX (Chandrayaan-5) is a joint mission between ISRO and JAXA to study the lunar South Pole, where ISRO is responsible for building the lander and JAXA is providing the rover.
evidenceExcerpt: LUPEX/Chandrayaan-5 is a joint ISRO-JAXA mission to study the lunar South Pole, with ISRO building the lander and JAXA providing the rover.
examUse: Statement-based questions testing knowledge of international space collaborations and specific agency contributions.
distractorHooks: Swapping the roles by stating ISRO provides the rover and JAXA provides the lander
Claiming LUPEX is an ISRO-NASA collaboration like NISAR
Stating the mission targets the lunar equator

claimText: NASA's ESCAPADE mission is designed to investigate the interactions between the solar wind and the magnetic environment of Mars.
evidenceExcerpt: ESCAPADE (NASA) investigates solar wind interactions with Mars' magnetic environment.
examUse: Match-the-following questions pairing interplanetary missions with their target planets and phenomena.
distractorHooks: Claiming ESCAPADE studies Venus or Earth's magnetosphere
Stating it is a mission to study the Sun directly

claimText: The Solar Orbiter is a collaborative mission between ESA and NASA aimed at capturing close-up images of the Sun.
evidenceExcerpt: Solar Orbiter (ESA & NASA): Close-up images of the Sun.
examUse: Correct-pair questions identifying joint space missions and their objectives.
distractorHooks: Claiming it is exclusively an ESA or exclusively a NASA mission
Confusing its objective with studying solar wind at Mars or mapping the heliosphere boundary
